$comment: PROTO-EARTH - the target of the giant impact, and a DIFFERENT BODY from modern Earth. Modern Earth is the RESULT: proto-Earth + Theia - the Moon - what escaped. Pointing the birth scene at earth.json produced a post-impact planet of 1.115 Earth masses, 11% too heavy, because it added a Mars-sized body to a planet that already included it. Mass here is 0.910 M_earth, which is what the budget demands (modern + Moon - Theia) and independently what the giant-impact literature gives (~0.9). Layer radii are modern Earth's scaled by (0.910)^(1/3) at unchanged densities - FLAGGED: a slightly less massive planet is also slightly less compressed, so the real proto-Earth's density profile differs a little from this. No atmosphere: modern air is a product of later outgassing and would not survive this impact anyway.
name: Proto-Earth
